LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: PLAINTIFF MARCO MARTINEZ'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AND LIFT STAY OF PROCEEDINGS IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 13, 2020 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE RICHARD ULMER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARCO MEDRANO MARTINEZ'S "MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AND LIFT STAY OF PROCEEDINGS" IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY THAT WOULD PERMIT THE ARBITRATION TO BE VACATED; CCP 1281.8(B) REGARDS PROVISIONAL REMEDIES DURING AN ARBITRATION, NOT VACATION. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar Apr-06-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS SUNRUN INC., SUNRUN INSTALLATION SERVICES INC.'S Joint Petition To Enforce Term Of Arbitration Agreement.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adoptes as follows:

The parties' joint petition to enforce term of arbitration agreement is granted. Pursuant to the parties' stipulation, and the terms of the arbitration agreement, the Court hereby proposes the following five arbitrators:

1. Pamela L. Hemminger, Esq.
2. Robert D. Coviello, Esq.
3. Dwight L. Armstrong, Esq.
4. Eric M. Epstein, Esq.
5. Steven J. Kaplan, Esq.

Each party may, within 5 days of the hearing, strike one name from the list by filing and serving a pleading with the Court. Thereafter, the Court will appoint as arbitrator a person whose name has not been struck from the list.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2020 Continued To Jun-16-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Arbitration And Lift Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Fatemeh Mashouf (213-620-1780) of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARCO MARTINEZ'S Motion To Vacate Arbitration And Lift Stay Of Proceedings

Plaintiff Marco Medrano Martinez's "motion to vacate arbitration and lift stay of proceedings" is denied. Plaintiff cites no authority that would permit the arbitration to be vacated; CCP 1281.8(b) regards provisional remedies during an arbitration, not vacation.
As to the stay: In late 2019, plaintiff sued defendants in a FEHA action (being arbitrated) and a wage and hour action. The latter invoked the Private Attorney General Act. Plaintiff stated: "On July 2, 2020, Plaintiff informed Defendants that he intended to bring his individual wage and hour claims into his arbitration matter." (7/24/20 Opp. 3:34.) This was an apparent efficiency measure to test the wage and hour claims in arbitration before expensive full-blown litigation. Plaintiff later reversed himself, but defendants referred the wage and hour claims to the arbitration. On September 18, 2020, defendants' motion to stay the litigation pending the arbitration was granted. The arbitration is ongoing, and plaintiff is prosecuting individual wage and hour "claims/counterclaims" in it. (10/23/20 Opp. 10:1-3.)
Plaintiff's argument on the current motion recycles his argument from last summer: "No 'individual wage and hour claims' are in dispute." (7/24/20 Opp. 3:18-6:6; 10/7/20 Memo 7:13-14.) This is flatly contrary to (1) plaintiff's July statement that he has "individual wage and hour claims," (2) his complaint, which states that he is suing as an "Individual and Representative" (id. at 3:3) and (3) his individual wage and hour claims filed in the arbitration.

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
